hints.text: ALL car coverage = **low-cam** (bigger blur, roads look wide, low angle)

shared_with.diff: FL plates; tiny country between CH and AT.

hints.text: Plates **fully white, NO blue strip**; front plate extremely short; rear long or tall w/ **red Swiss emblem left + canton emblem right**
Bollards black-white w/ white/grey reflectors — wedge or cylindrical w/ rounded top (Liechtenstein nearly identical)

shared_with.diff: FL plates + Vaduz castle views → Liechtenstein.

hints.text: Directional signs: **blue = main road, white = side road**; road signs often in a **round grey metal border**; town signs white/blue w/ metal frame (back: distances + canton abbreviation)
Pedestrian signs: **7 stripes on a tall blue rectangle** (Europe: only CH+FL)
hints.uniq_note: Liechtenstein uses identical signs — everything else in Europe differs.

hints.text: **Long dashed yellow lines** + **yellow pedestrian crossings** (Europe: only CH+FL)
hints.uniq_note: Yellow crossings/dashes = Switzerland or Liechtenstein only.

hints.text: Chevrons **black w/ white arrow** (rarely inverted; Austria = red-white/red-yellow)
South = extremely mountainous w/ thin valleys; north = farmland on flat/rolling ground w/ forested hills
Alpine: light warm stone + wood, gable tiled roofs, **two-part horizontal wooden shutters**
Bus-stop signs w/ **thick metal post looping around them** — unique to Switzerland
**4 languages** (German/French/Italian/Romansh) by region; Swiss German has **NO ß**; speed-sign text language matches the region
**Canton 2-letter abbreviations** on town signs + unblurred plates; postal-code first digit increases **west → east**
**Jura**: flatter land w/ long forested medium ridge NW; exposed limestone from forested hills; rock walls (also Italian-speaking Alps)

**Architecture regions**: Ticino = Italian style (smooth orange-yellow stone, IT shutters); Bern area = thick arch-shaped roofs; northeast = elaborate **red timber frames**; Appenzell = house+barn at 90° w/ square-grid facades; Graubünden = all-stone houses w/ white painted window decor

**Canton infra**: Vaud = large town signs; Basel-Landschaft = **olive-green** posts/borders/backs; Valais = thick wooden bollards; Glarus = reflectors on fences instead of bollards; Geneva (+Martigny valley) = simplified bike symbol w/ disconnected wheels; road-divider types mapped

**Lake tells**: hilly + big lake south w/ tall mountains behind = north of **Lake Geneva**; wide lake NE in low terrain = **Lake Constance**; Martigny valley = highest fruit-plantation density (also near Constance & NW of Geneva)
**Bernina-Express train coverage** in Graubünden (down to Tirano/Italy); Gen2 only near the Bern/Valais border + short Schaffhausen bits (+Gen2 ski resorts in the south)

**Sankt Gallen** = blue street signs w/ rounded side edges (unique); **Basel** = big blue signs on low double posts (Denmark-like); **Bern** downtown = grey 4-storey buildings w/ rounded arcade openings
